prompt:
The bike repair-counter app is completely down this morning — it won't even start, before a single order can be taken. A co-op student did a 'cleanup pass' across the whole tree on Friday and left for the weekend. First thing the run shows:

  Traceback (most recent call last):
    File "test_spokeshop.py", line 6, in test_rate_card
      from pricing import RateCard
    File "pricing.py", line 4, in <module>
      class RateCard:
      ...
    File "pricing.py", line 5, in RateCard
      WEEKEND = dict(BASE, surcharge=1500)
                     ^^^^
  NameError: name 'BASE' is not defined

and it is NOT just that one file — each thing we patch gets us one module further and something else blows up on import or first use; even the nightly JSON export line came out broken when we got that far by hand, with some nonsense about the json module having no attribute 'dumps'. Nothing about the BUSINESS rules changed on Friday (rates, ticket wording, bay policy, intake steps, the export format) and `python3 test_spokeshop.py` pins all of it. Please get the suite green. Don't touch the test file, and keep the fixes surgical — we want Friday's code, working, not a rewrite.
notes: Six runtime-immediate planted errors staged as 'won't start', spread one-per-file (import-time failures reveal in test order — chain-reveals intended and documented per family-5C law): (1) pricing.py class attribute WEEKEND references BASE before its definition in the class body (NameError at import); (2) intake.py module-level dispatch table built above the step functions it names (NameError at import); (3) orders.py decorator typo @vaildator for @validator (NameError at import); (4) tickets.py WarrantyTicket passes claim_no through super().__init__ whose base takes two args (TypeError at first instantiation; tests also pin .claim_no so the fix must assign it); (5) bays.py mutable default `taken=[]` — the crash path is pinned: four bare calls across separate 'days' must each yield B1, but the shared default fills the rack and the fourth raises RuntimeError; explicit-list threading behavior is pinned separately so the fix must keep appending to caller lists; (6) json.py — shop JSON helpers named after the stdlib module; with the workspace as sys.path[0] its own `import json` self-imports, so order_line dies with AttributeError: module 'json' has no attribute 'dumps'; reference fix renames it shopjson.py and updates the orders.py import. Tests are ordered pricing -> tickets -> bays -> intake -> orders so each fix reveals the next failure deterministically.
